questions.explanation: Cocaine and mandrax are illicit drugs that cause long-term body damage when abused, unlike cocoa, fancida (an antimalarial), and panadol which are not illicit harmful drugs.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the list below.
1 Pubic hair grows
2 Morning sickness
3 Appearance of pimples
4 Enlargement of breasts
5 Breaking of the voice
The numbers showing features of pregnancy are...
questions.explanation: Morning sickness and enlargement of breasts are features associated with pregnancy, while pubic hair growth, pimples, and voice breaking are features of puberty, not pregnancy.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: What movements of the earth cause seasons, days and nights?

options.A: Seasons: Revolution, Day and Night: Rotation
options.B: Seasons: Rotation, Day and Night: Revolution
options.C: Seasons: Revolution, Day and Night: Revolution
options.D: Seasons: Rotation, Day and Night: Rotation

questions.explanation: The Earth's revolution around the sun causes seasons, while its rotation on its own axis causes day and night.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following substances can ruin the lives of people if abused?
questions.explanation: Dagga (marijuana) is an illicit substance that can ruin people's lives if abused, unlike bruffen, caffeine, and panadol which are common, legal substances.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the procedures below is followed in the extraction and purification of copper in Zambia?
questions.explanation: The correct sequence in copper extraction and purification is: crushing, floating (floatation/concentration), smelting, and then electrolysis to refine the copper.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following methods of communication uses sound?
questions.explanation: Mobile phones and drums both use sound to communicate (voice calls and drum beats), unlike e-mail, fax, and letters which are visual/written forms of communication.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following are items made from copper?
questions.explanation: Copper is commonly used to make coins and jewellery due to its malleability and attractive appearance, unlike permanent magnets, hoes, roofing sheets, and vehicle batteries which are typically made from other materials.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
The diagram below shows a small box.

(Diagram shows dimensions: 4cm height, 2cm width, 5cm length)

What is the volume of the box?
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height = 5cm × 2cm × 4cm = 40cm³.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
During an experiment, learners used solid materials as shown in the diagram below.

(Diagram shows rods 1: iron rod, 2: wooden rod, 3: copper rod, 4: glass rod, each with wax attached and heated by a candle)

In which of the rods above will the wax melt?
questions.explanation: Iron and copper are good conductors of heat, so heat travels along these rods and melts the wax, unlike wood and glass which are poor conductors (insulators) of heat.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: A person who cannot stop abusing drugs is...
questions.explanation: A person who cannot stop abusing drugs is described as an addict, reflecting a dependency on the substance.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the list of fertilizers below.
1 Dead plants
2 Green manure
3 Animal waste
4 Compound D
Which of the fertilizers is not organic?
questions.explanation: Compound D is a chemical (inorganic) fertiliser, unlike dead plants, green manure, and animal waste, which are all organic fertilisers derived from living or once-living material.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the pie chart below showing composition of air.

(Pie chart shows sections labeled G, H, F, and E, with E being the largest section)

The gas represented by the part marked E is...
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up the largest percentage of air (about 78%), corresponding to the largest section E in the pie chart.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: What do we call animals which destroy certain crops by feeding on them?
questions.explanation: Animals that destroy crops by feeding on them are called pests.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following consists of properties of copper?
questions.explanation: Copper is a good conductor of electricity and is resistant to rust (corrosion), making 'conductivity and resistant' the correct pairing of its properties.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The normal human body temperature is...
questions.explanation: The normal human body temperature is approximately 37°C.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is an insulator?
questions.explanation: Plastic is an insulator, meaning it does not conduct electricity, unlike aluminium, copper, and silver which are good conductors of electricity.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Where in the alimentary canal are nutrients and water absorbed?

options.B: Nutrients: Small intestine, Water: Large intestine

questions.explanation: Nutrients are absorbed in the small intestine, while water is mainly absorbed in the large intestine.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: A Grade 7 learner wanted to obtain salt from a mixture of salt and sand. What process did the learner use to achieve this?
questions.explanation: To separate salt from a mixture of salt and sand, the mixture is first dissolved in water and filtered to remove the sand, then the salt solution is evaporated to recover the salt.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following methods can produce a high pitch of sound?

options.C: Hitting a filled up container of water

questions.explanation: Hitting a metal bar produces a high pitch of sound due to its high frequency vibrations, unlike the other actions which produce lower or duller sounds.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a simple machine used to hold things together?
questions.explanation: A screw is a simple machine used to hold things together, unlike an inclined plane, pulley, and seesaw which serve other functions such as lifting and moving objects.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: One of the advantages of chemical fertilizer in agriculture is that it...

options.B: improves the water retention of the soil.
options.D: supplies nutrients to plants over a longer period.

questions.explanation: Chemical fertilisers supply nutrients to plants quickly, allowing for fast nutrient uptake, unlike organic fertilisers which release nutrients more slowly.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a list of non metals?
questions.explanation: Carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, and carbon dioxide are all non-metals, unlike the other options which include aluminium and/or zinc, which are metals.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the diagram of the alimentary canal below.

(Diagram shows parts labeled 1, 2, 3, 4 - esophagus, liver area, large intestine, small intestine)

In which of the labelled parts does absorption of food take place?
questions.explanation: Part 4 in the diagram represents the small intestine, where most absorption of digested food takes place.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: What is the advantage of keeping livestock together?

options.A: Diseases are more likely to spread
options.B: Diseases are not likely to spread

questions.explanation: Keeping livestock together makes it easier for farmers to manage, monitor, and care for them, despite the increased risk of disease spread, which is a separate disadvantage.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not a way of retaining water in the soil?
questions.explanation: Spraying is not a method of retaining water in the soil; mulching, shading, and terracing all help retain soil moisture by reducing evaporation or runoff.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Food which is not digested in the alimentary canal is...
questions.explanation: Undigested food material that passes through the alimentary canal is eventually eliminated from the body as faeces.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a source of light in the solar system?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the source of light in the solar system; Earth, Mars, and the Moon only reflect light from the Sun.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following lists.
1 Iron, Zinc, Copper, Aluminium
2 Carbon, Hydrogen, Zinc, Carbon dioxide
3 Iron, Zinc, Hydrogen, Oxygen
4 Carbon dioxide, Copper, Zinc, Oxygen
Which list consists of metals only?
questions.explanation: Iron, zinc, copper, and aluminium are all metals, unlike the other lists which include non-metals such as carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, or carbon dioxide.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following are skin diseases?
questions.explanation: Measles, scabies, and chicken pox are all diseases that affect the skin, unlike the other options which mix in unrelated conditions such as scurvy (deficiency disease) and marasmus (malnutrition).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is the best way of looking after HIV and AIDS patients?

options.A: Give them good nutrition and love
options.B: Let them use their own cups
options.D: Support but isolate them from the community

questions.explanation: Giving HIV and AIDS patients good nutrition and love is the best way to care for them, unlike isolating them or restricting their rights, which causes unnecessary stigma and harm.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Many cooking pots are made of metals instead of clay because a metal is...
questions.explanation: Metal is a better conductor of heat than clay, allowing pots to heat up quickly and evenly for cooking.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following are needs of livestock?
questions.explanation: Food and water are essential basic needs of livestock, unlike the other pairings which are not both essential needs in the same way.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
What is the volume of the following object?

(Diagram shows dimensions: 4cm, 2cm, 2cm)
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height = 4cm × 2cm × 2cm = 16cm³.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: One of the methods of conserving animal species from becoming extinct is by ...

options.A: keeping the animals in game parks.
options.D: killing some animals for a period of time.

questions.explanation: Keeping animals in game parks protects them from poaching and habitat loss, helping to conserve animal species from becoming extinct.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: What do doctors use to determine the body temperature of patients?
questions.explanation: A thermometer is the instrument doctors use to measure the body temperature of patients.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Excessive use of alcohol harms the body by causing...
questions.explanation: Excessive alcohol consumption is well known to cause liver damage.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not a ventilator?
questions.explanation: A plain roof is not a ventilator; air vents, doors, and windows allow air to circulate in and out of a building, unlike a solid roof.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram.

(Bar graph showing amount of rainfall in cm³ for Mon-Sun: Mon 5, Tue 10, Wed 5, Thu 9.5, Fri 3, Sat 5, Sun 15)

On which day was the lowest rainfall recorded?
questions.explanation: Friday recorded the lowest rainfall amount (approximately 3 cm³) compared to all other days shown in the bar graph.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: In the water cycle, what is the change from liquid to gas state called?
questions.explanation: Evaporation is the change from liquid to gas state in the water cycle.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Why are ventilators important in a building?
questions.explanation: Ventilators are important in a building because they allow free air circulation, ensuring fresh air movement and removal of stale air.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The property of Copper that makes it useful all over the world is its ...
questions.explanation: Copper's good conductivity (of electricity and heat) is the property that makes it useful worldwide, particularly in electrical applications.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not a deficiency disease?
questions.explanation: Dysentery is an infectious disease caused by bacteria or parasites, not a nutritional deficiency disease, unlike kwashiorkor, marasmus, and rickets which result from nutrient deficiencies.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram.

(Diagram shows a measuring cylinder with scale 1-5)

What does the above apparatus measure?
questions.explanation: The apparatus shown is a measuring cylinder, which is used to measure volume.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Farmers are advised not to crowd their animals because ...
questions.explanation: Crowding animals increases the risk of diseases spreading easily among them, which is why farmers are advised against it.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following organs is not part of the digestive system?
questions.explanation: The kidneys are part of the excretory system, not the digestive system, unlike the large intestine, pancreas, and small intestine which are all digestive organs.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Grade 5 learners left plant materials in a pit to decompose in order to make ...
questions.explanation: Decomposing plant materials in a pit produces compost manure, an organic fertiliser.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following list of food types.
1 Pumpkin
2 Mango
3 Pawpaw
4 Carrot
The list contains food rich in...
questions.explanation: Pumpkin, mango, pawpaw, and carrot are all fruits/vegetables rich in vitamins.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: A farmer keeping cattle cleaned the kraal twice a week in order to prevent ...

options.C: bad smell of animal urine and dung.
options.D: dust from settling in the kraal.

questions.explanation: Cleaning the kraal regularly primarily helps prevent the outbreak of diseases among the cattle by maintaining hygiene.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is the difference between organic and inorganic fertilizers?

options.A: Organic: Made from natural material, Inorganic: Made from chemicals
options.B: Organic: Sold as granules in bags, Inorganic: Sold as manure
options.C: Organic: Very expensive, Inorganic: Very cheap
options.D: Organic: Small amounts required, Inorganic: Large amounts must be used

questions.explanation: Organic fertilisers are made from natural materials (such as plant or animal matter), while inorganic fertilisers are made from chemicals, which is the correct distinguishing characteristic.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Communication is important because it...
questions.explanation: Communication is important because it helps to pass messages between people.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
The following diagram shows a simple machine.

(Diagram shows scissors with parts labeled 1, 2, 3, 4)

Which numbered part represents the pivot?
questions.explanation: Part 2 in the scissors diagram represents the pivot point, the fixed point around which the blades rotate.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following animals is classified as a mammal?
questions.explanation: A cow is classified as a mammal, unlike a duck (bird), frog (amphibian), and lizard (reptile).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following experiment showing a property of matter.

(Diagram shows a bottle submerged in a dish of water with bubbles rising)

The experiment shows that air ...
questions.explanation: Bubbles of air escaping from the inverted bottle as water enters demonstrate that air occupies space, since the air had to be displaced for water to fill the bottle.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Some children who were left with a brazier inside a poorly ventilated room suffocated but were saved by quickly ...

options.A: blowing air on them using a fan.
options.C: taking them out for fresh air.

questions.explanation: Taking suffocating children out of a poorly ventilated room into fresh air is the immediate, life-saving action needed to address oxygen deprivation/carbon monoxide poisoning.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Dust from quarrying and mine dumps affects the environment by causing ... pollution.
questions.explanation: Dust from quarrying and mine dumps causes air pollution (dust particles in the air) and land pollution (dust settling on land), unlike the other pairings which include noise or water, which are not the direct effects of dust specifically.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Sound can be made louder by...
questions.explanation: Making vibrations stronger increases the amplitude of sound waves, making the sound louder.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram of the digestive system.

What happens in the part labelled X?
questions.explanation: Part X in the diagram represents the small intestine, where absorption of digested food (nutrients) takes place.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram.

(Diagram A shows a Eureka can with a stone submerged in water on a string, overflowing into B, a measuring cylinder showing 10cm³ of displaced water)

What is the volume of the stone?
questions.explanation: The volume of water displaced and collected in the measuring cylinder (10cm³) equals the volume of the stone, by the principle of water displacement.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Learners carried out an investigation to find out whether the following substances were conductors or insulators.
- Plastic
- Rubber
- Paper
The above were discovered that they were ...
questions.explanation: Plastic, rubber, and paper are all bad conductors (insulators) of electricity.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following plants is propagated using suckers?
questions.explanation: Bananas are propagated using suckers, which grow from the base of the parent plant, unlike potatoes (tubers), maize (seeds), and cassava (stem cuttings).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is an alternative energy source in view of load shedding?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is a practical and commonly adopted alternative energy source for individual households and businesses to address load shedding (since hydro is typically the main grid source affected by load shedding itself).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
The following diagram shows an animal.

(Diagram shows a lizard-like animal)

The animal above is a ...
questions.explanation: The lizard shown in the diagram is a vertebrate, as it has a backbone; it is not a mammal (it's a reptile), not warm-blooded (reptiles are cold-blooded), and not a wasp (which is an insect).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not an elicit drug?
questions.explanation: Panadol is a legal, common pain relief medicine, unlike alcohol (legal but commonly abused), cocaine, and heroin, which are illicit drugs.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following correctly gives the age at which boys and girls reach puberty?
questions.explanation: Boys generally reach puberty around age 11, while girls reach puberty slightly earlier around age 10, reflecting the typical earlier onset of puberty in girls compared to boys.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
A learner described an animal as having the following features.
- moist skin
- cold blooded
- lives on land and in water
Which type of animal did the learner describe?
questions.explanation: Moist skin, cold-bloodedness, and living both on land and in water are characteristic features of amphibians.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which diseases are caused by tsetse flies in man and in cattle?

options.B: Man: Dysentery, Cattle: Foot and mouth disease

questions.explanation: Tsetse flies cause sleeping sickness in humans and nagana in cattle, both forms of trypanosomiasis transmitted by the same insect.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Why is lightning seen first before the thunder is heard?
questions.explanation: Light travels much faster than sound, so the flash of lightning is seen before the sound of thunder is heard, even though both occur simultaneously.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram.

(Diagram shows a tool with a wooden handle and metal end resting on a pivot)

What is the simple machine shown in the diagram?
questions.explanation: The tool shown, with a handle resting on a pivot/fulcrum to lift or move objects, is a lever.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following are ways of preventing the spread of HIV and AIDS?

options.A: Abstain from sex and avoid shaking hands.
options.B: Avoid drug abuse and HIV positive people.
options.C: Eat a balanced diet and sleep under a mosquito net.
options.D: Not sharing combs and razor blades.

questions.explanation: Not sharing combs and razor blades helps prevent HIV transmission through blood contact, unlike the other options which include irrelevant or unrelated actions (avoiding handshakes, avoiding HIV positive people entirely, sleeping under mosquito nets).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following first aid procedure.
1 Put the victim in an airy place
2 Use mouth to mouth resuscitation
3 Take the victim to a clinic or hospital
Which accident may prompt you to follow the laid down first aid procedure?
questions.explanation: Suffocation requires the described first aid procedure (fresh air, mouth-to-mouth resuscitation, medical attention) to restore breathing, unlike choking, electric shock, or snake bite which require different specific interventions.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The movement of blood in the body is called ...
questions.explanation: The movement of blood throughout the body is called circulation.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram.

(Diagram shows a battery symbol with multiple cells)

The use of the above in a circuit is to ...

options.B: join the wires in a circuit.

questions.explanation: The battery (cells) shown in the diagram is used in a circuit to provide electric energy, powering the circuit.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Musical instruments and decorative objects are made from a combination of copper and zinc which is called ...
questions.explanation: Brass is an alloy made from a combination of copper and zinc, commonly used for musical instruments and decorative objects.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following table showing metals and non-metals.

Metals: Aluminium, Carbon, Copper, Iron
Non-Metals: Hydrogen, Carbon dioxide, Oxygen, Zinc

Which of the following substances are placed in a wrong column?
questions.explanation: Carbon is actually a non-metal (placed incorrectly under Metals) and zinc is actually a metal (placed incorrectly under Non-Metals), so 'Carbon and zinc' are the substances placed in the wrong columns.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not a health risk associated with early sexual debut?
questions.explanation: Stopping of the menstrual period is not a health risk associated with early sexual debut; cervical cancer, STIs/HIV, and teenage pregnancy are all known health risks linked to early sexual activity.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: During the test for starch, the brown colour indicates that there is...
questions.explanation: In the iodine starch test, a brown colour (the original colour of iodine solution) indicates the absence of starch, since starch would normally turn the solution blue/black.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following pairs of diseases affect the skin?
questions.explanation: Scabies and chicken pox are both diseases that affect the skin, unlike the other pairs which mix in diseases that affect other body systems (typhoid, bilharzia, flu).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following wedges is used to cut firewood?
questions.explanation: An axe is a wedge-shaped tool specifically used to cut firewood, unlike a knife, nail, or screw which serve other purposes.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following consists of metals only?
questions.explanation: Iron, copper, and zinc are all metals, unlike the other options which include glass, plastic, or clay, which are non-metal materials.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The process by which food is broken down into nutrients that body cells use is called ...
questions.explanation: Digestion is the process by which food is broken down into nutrients that the body's cells can use.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following methods of communication uses sound?
questions.explanation: A drum communicates through sound (beats), unlike email, fax, and letters which are written/visual forms of communication.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a component of air?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is a gas and a component of air, unlike aluminium, carbon, and zinc which are solid elements/metals (carbon, while present as carbon dioxide gas, as a pure element is solid).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a component in an electric circuit?
questions.explanation: A connecting wire is a physical component used in an electric circuit, unlike current (which is a flow of charge, not a component), and a heater/pressing iron (which are appliances powered by a circuit, not components of it).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The volume of a liquid is measured using a ...
questions.explanation: A measuring cylinder is the correct instrument used to measure the volume of a liquid.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The best way to conserve electricity is by ...

options.B: switching appliances off when not in use.
options.C: using electricity for cooking every time.

questions.explanation: Switching off appliances when not in use is the best way to conserve electricity, as it reduces unnecessary power consumption.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: When a person becomes dependent on a drug, it is said that the person is ...
questions.explanation: A person who becomes dependent on a drug is described as addicted.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The function of the heart in the body is to ...
questions.explanation: The heart's function is to pump blood throughout the body.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram of a fish.

Which labelled part of the fish is used for swimming?
questions.explanation: Part W in the fish diagram points to the fins, which the fish uses for swimming, unlike X (mouth area), Y (dorsal fin used for stability), and Z (tail fin used more for steering/propulsion in combination with body movement, though fins overall as a group are used for swimming, W specifically representing the main propulsion fin here).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following lists consists of food rich in protein?
questions.explanation: Meat, beans, and fish are all good sources of protein, unlike the other options which are fruits, sugars, or carbohydrates.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Malaria can be treated by using ...
questions.explanation: Coartem is an antimalarial medicine specifically used to treat malaria, unlike aspirin, b-complex, and panadol which are not antimalarial drugs.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following gases will build up in an overcrowded room when all windows and doors are closed?
questions.explanation: In an overcrowded, poorly ventilated room, carbon dioxide builds up because people exhale it during respiration and it cannot escape without proper ventilation.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a vertebrate?
questions.explanation: A lizard is a vertebrate (reptile) with a backbone, unlike a snail, butterfly, and bee, which are all invertebrates.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Girls exposed to early sexual contact are most likely to ...

options.A: live longer than their life expectancy.

questions.explanation: Early sexual contact increases the risk of girls developing cervical cancer, among other health risks, unlike the other options which are not associated outcomes of early sexual activity.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The advantage of chemical fertilisers is that they ...
questions.explanation: Chemical fertilisers release plant nutrients quickly, allowing for fast nutrient uptake by plants; the other options describe disadvantages of chemical fertilisers, not advantages.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: One way of conserving water is by ...

options.A: always using a garden tap only.
options.B: leaving the well open all the time.
options.C: turning off taps when not in use.
options.D: washing clothes as often as they are dirty.

questions.explanation: Turning off taps when not in use prevents unnecessary water wastage, conserving water.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is not a way of providing ventilation in a building?
questions.explanation: A roof, on its own, is not a means of providing ventilation; fans, vents, and windows allow air to circulate, unlike a plain roof.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is the best way to prevent the spread of HIV and AIDS?
questions.explanation: Abstinence is the most effective way to prevent the spread of HIV and AIDS, as it eliminates the risk of sexual transmission entirely, whereas the other options reduce but do not eliminate other specific transmission routes.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: What is likely to happen when animals are overcrowded on a farm?
questions.explanation: Overcrowding animals on a farm increases the likelihood of disease outbreaks due to close contact and stress among the animals.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
The diagram shows a simple machine.

What is the name of the simple machine shown in the diagram?
questions.explanation: The diagram shows a sloped ramp used to move an object to a higher level, which is an inclined plane.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is the difference between insects and spiders?

options.A: INSECTS: Eight legs, SPIDERS: Six legs
options.B: INSECTS: No wings, SPIDERS: Has wings
options.C: INSECTS: Six legs, SPIDERS: Eight legs
options.D: INSECTS: Two segmented body, SPIDERS: Three segmented body

questions.explanation: Insects have six legs while spiders have eight legs, which is the correct distinguishing characteristic between the two groups.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following methods can be used to separate iron fillings from sand?
questions.explanation: Magnetism is used to separate iron filings from sand, as iron is magnetic and sand is not.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: A bat is classified as a mammal because it ...
questions.explanation: A bat is classified as a mammal because it gives birth to live young ones and feeds them with milk, a defining characteristic of mammals.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The best practice of improving water holding capacity in a sandy garden is by adding to seed beds ...
questions.explanation: Adding compost manure improves the water holding capacity of sandy soil by adding organic matter, which helps retain moisture.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following tools exerts the most pressure?
questions.explanation: A needle has a very small surface area at its sharp point, which concentrates force into a tiny area, exerting the most pressure among the listed items.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
The diagram shows a type of seed dispersal.

(Diagram shows a pod with labels: Pericarp, Seed, Remains of style and stigma, Remains of sepals)

What type of seed dispersal is shown in the diagram?
questions.explanation: The pod-like fruit structure shown, with a pericarp that dries and splits to release seeds, is characteristic of self (explosive) seed dispersal.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a sign of dysentery?
questions.explanation: Diarrhoea with blood is a characteristic sign of dysentery.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: A salt lick in an animal's diet is important because it supplies ...
questions.explanation: A salt lick supplies essential minerals to animals, which are needed to supplement their diet, especially in areas where the natural diet is deficient in certain minerals.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is a drug that is harmful to the body?
questions.explanation: Mandrax is an illicit drug that is harmful to the body, unlike chloroquine, penicillin, and panadol which are legitimate medicines.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following diagram representing a process taking place in a leaf.

(Diagram shows a leaf with Gas X entering, Gas Y leaving, and sunlight entering)

Which of the following best identifies the process, gas X and gas Y?

options.A: Process: Evaporation, Gas X: Oxygen, Gas Y: Nitrogen
options.B: Process: Photosynthesis, Gas X: Carbon dioxide, Gas Y: Oxygen
options.C: Process: Respiration, Gas X: Carbon dioxide, Gas Y: Oxygen
options.D: Process: Transpiration, Gas X: Nitrogen, Gas Y: Carbon dioxide

questions.explanation: The process occurring in the leaf with sunlight present is photosynthesis, where the leaf takes in carbon dioxide (Gas X) and releases oxygen (Gas Y).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following metals is used for making bodies of aeroplanes?
questions.explanation: Aluminium is widely used for making aeroplane bodies due to its lightweight yet strong properties.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following pairs describes the signs of pregnancy?

options.B: High temperature and enlargement of breasts
options.C: Morning sickness and stoppage of menstruation
options.D: Stomach ache and bulging of the tummy

questions.explanation: Morning sickness and stoppage of menstruation are well-known, reliable early signs of pregnancy.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The importance of insects in crop production is that they play a role in ...

options.D: provision of protein in the diet.

questions.explanation: Insects play an important role in crop production through plant pollination, transferring pollen between flowers to enable fertilisation and fruit/seed formation.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The function of platelets is to ...
questions.explanation: Platelets function to clot blood, helping to stop bleeding when blood vessels are damaged.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: How are sweet potatoes propagated?
questions.explanation: Sweet potatoes are commonly propagated through stem cutting (using vine cuttings), unlike budding and grafting which are used for other plants such as fruit trees.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Cattle are not reared in some parts of Zambia due to ...
questions.explanation: Tsetse fly infestation prevents cattle rearing in some parts of Zambia, as the flies transmit trypanosomiasis (nagana), a disease fatal to cattle.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: What is the normal body temperature of the human body?
questions.explanation: The normal human body temperature is approximately 37°C.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following are components of blood?
questions.explanation: Platelets and plasma are both components of blood, unlike arteries, veins, and the heart, which are parts of the circulatory system but not components of blood itself.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Where is water absorbed in the alimentary canal?
questions.explanation: Water is mainly absorbed in the large intestines of the alimentary canal.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The process by which water changes from gas to liquid is called ...
questions.explanation: Condensation is the process by which water vapour (gas) changes into liquid water.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Cow dung is sometimes added in the compost heap in order to ...

options.A: ensure even decaying of the compost materials.
options.B: introduce decay micro-organisms into the compost heap.
options.C: prevent water logging in the compost heap.
options.D: separate the layers of the compost heap.

questions.explanation: Cow dung is added to a compost heap to introduce decay micro-organisms, which help break down the compost materials faster.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: Which of the following is the unit for mass?
questions.explanation: Kilograms is the unit used to measure mass, unlike amperes (electric current), centimetres (length), and newtons (force/weight).

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: A cooking stick is an example of a ... of heat.
questions.explanation: A cooking stick (typically made of wood) is a poor conductor of heat, which is why it does not get hot quickly and is safe to hold while cooking.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
Study the following list of water treatment processes.
1 Boiling
2 Filtration
3 Decantation
4 Chlorination
Which of the processes is used to remove suspended substances from water?
questions.explanation: Filtration is the process used to remove suspended substances from water by passing it through a filter medium.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: The importance of copper in Zambia is that it is a ...

options.B: source of raw materials for agriculture.

questions.explanation: Copper is an important source of foreign exchange for Zambia, as it is a major export commodity that generates revenue from international trade.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question:
The diagram shows a box.

(Diagram shows dimensions: 10cm, 12cm, 6cm)

What is the volume of the box?
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height = 12cm × 10cm × 6cm = 720cm³.

questions.subject: Integrated Science
questions.question: A patient was asked to include food rich in iron in the diet because the patient had ...
questions.explanation: Iron-rich foods are recommended for patients with anaemia, as iron is essential for the production of haemoglobin in red blood cells.
